1. Maximum shear stress and principle stress will be same

Solution

The maximum shear stress is zero, while the principal stress is the value of the normal stress, which is constant but not necessarily zero.

Q15 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about various types of fluid acceleration in a flow:
1. The tangential acceleration is developed for a fluid particle when the magnitude of the velocity changes with respect to space and time.
2. Normal acceleration is developed when a fluid particle moves in a curved path along which the direction of the velocity is changing.
Which of the statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Tangential component arises if there is change in the magnitude of velocity with respect to time, space or both. It is tangential to stream line.
• Normal component arises when the direction of velocity changes w.r.t time, space or both.
This happens when fluid is moving along a curved path. It is normal to stream line.

2. Mercury is used to measure the atmospheric pressure because it has high vapour pressure.
3. Atmospheric pressure is measured by measuring the height h' in the altimeter.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are incorrect?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• To measure atmospheric pressure, a mercury filled tube is inverted into a mercury container that is open to atmosphere. It is measured by measuring the height 'h' in the barometer
• Altimeter is an instrument used to measured the altitude of an object above a fixed level.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

For a cylindrical block completely submerged under water, the only correct statement among the following is:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• According to Archimedes principle, the buoyant force on an object completely submerged in a fluid is equal to the weight of the fluid it displaces.
• The weight of the completely submerged body is the same regardless of its orientation.

Q28 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Virtual work means:
1. Work done by real forces due to hypothetical displacement.
2. Work done by hypothetical forces during real displacement.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Work done by real forces due to hypothetical displacements: This is the most common
definition of virtual work and is used to find unknown forces or solve equilibrium problems.
• Work done by hypothetical forces during real displacements: This is also a valid application
of the virtual work principle, often used in a method called the "unit dummy force method"
to calculate real displacements. This method applies a hypothetical (virtual) unit force to the
point where the displacement is desired and then calculates the work done by this force as
the structure deforms under its actual loads.

Q38 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider following types of loads:
1. Gravity loads
2. Lateral loads
3. Wind loads
4. Longitudinal loads
Of the above loads, gantry girders are designed for

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Gantry girders are designed to resist the following loads associated with the operation of an
overhead crane:
• Gravity loads: These are the vertical forces from the weight of the girder itself, the crane
bridge, and the lifted load. This is the primary vertical load the girder must support.
• Lateral loads: These are horizontal forces caused by the acceleration or braking of the crab
(trolley) as it moves across the crane bridge. They act perpendicular to the length of the
girder.
• Longitudinal loads: These are horizontal forces caused by the acceleration or braking of the
entire crane as it moves along the gantry rails. They act parallel to the length of the girder.

The block shear failure of a bolted joint in tension occurs because of
1. Use of high shear strength bolts

Solution

(c)

high strength bolts which results in fewer bolts and smaller connection lengths.

Q53 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding deep beams:
1. Ratio of effective span to overall depth is less than 2 for simply supported beam.
2. Ratio of effective span to overall depth is less than 2 for continuous beam.
3. Deep beams are designed for shear only.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are incorrect?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
As per IS 456 : 2000, Clause 25.1, a beam shall be called as a deep beam when the ratio of effective span to overall depth, l/D is less than
1. 2.0 for simply supported beam and,
2. 2.5 for continuous beam.
Deep beams are designed for bending moment and checked for shear.

Q55 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In a cantilever retaining wall, the main steel reinforcement in heel slab is provided

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Generally, downward pressure is more than the upward pressure and hence, main reinforcement
is provided on the bottom face of the slab but main reinforcement is provided whether on the top
face or bottom face depends upon the net resultant stress.

Q56 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Critical section for bending moment in the footing under masonry wall is located at midway
between the face and middle of the wall whereas in case of RC column, critical section is located
at the face of column.

Q64 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Correct sequence of preparation of clay for manufacturing of bricks is:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Unsoiling: The top layer of soil (about 200 mm) is removed because it contains more impurities.
• Digging: The remaining soil is then dug out and spread over a level field.
• Cleaning: Any pebbles, stones, and vegetable matter are removed from the dug clay.
• Weathering: The cleaned clay is exposed to the atmosphere for several weeks or months to mellow and soften it.

Q65 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which on of the following is employed to determine strength of hardened existing concrete structure?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Rebound Hammer Test: This is a non-destructive test used to estimate the compressive
strength of hardened concrete by measuring the rebound of a spring-loaded hammer after it
impacts the concrete's surface. The rebound value is then correlated to the concrete's strength.
• Cone penetration test: Cone penetration test is used to determine the consistency of mortar
and not the strength of hardened concrete.
• Kelly-ball test: This test is used to measure the workability or consistency of fresh concrete.
• Le-chatelier's test: This test is used to determine the soundness of cement, which is its ability to maintain a stable volume after setting and hardening.

Q66 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Enamel paints are solvent based coatings which are based on synthetic alkyl resins and have a higher level of gloss than ordinary paints.
2. Cement paint contains white cement as the common base.
Which of the above statements are CORRECT?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Enamel paints are coatings known for their hard, glossy, and durable finish. The synthetic
variety is typically solvent-based and uses alkyd resins as the binder, which provides these
characteristics and results in a higher gloss level than ordinary wall paints.
• Cement paint is a type of powder paint used for exterior masonry surfaces. It is made with
white cement as the common base, along with other ingredients such as pigments, fillers, and
additives. The addition of white cement gives the paint its specific properties and appearance.

2. Soundness test of cement is carried out to determine its free lime content.
Which of the above statements is(are) CORRECT?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Magnesium oxide or magnesia (MgO) when present in the range of 0.1-3% imparts hardness and
colour to the cement. A high content of magnesia makes the cement unsound.

Q73 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. CPM is event-oriented and adopts deterministic approach
2. PERT is event-oriented and adopts probabilistic approach
3. PERT is activity oriented and adopts probabilistic approach
Which of these statement(s) is/are INCORRECT?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
PERT is event-oriented and adopts probabilistic approach. The Program Evaluation and Review
Technique (PERT) focuses on project milestones or events. It uses a probabilistic approach with
three-time estimates for each activity (optimistic, most likely and pessimistic) to account for
uncertainty in project scheduling.

Q82 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which technique of stabilization for subbase is preferred for heavy plastic soils?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The preferred stabilization technique for heavy plastic soils is lime stabilization. Lime reacts with clay minerals in a process called pozzolanic reaction, which reduces plasticity, increases strength and durability, and improves workability.

Q83 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q84 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The actual shape of isochrones depends on:
1. The initial distribution of excess pore pressure
2. The height of the sample
3. The change in effective stress of the sample
4. The drainage conditions (one way or two way)
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The shape of isochrones is determined by the conditions that govern the dissipation of excess
pore water pressure during consolidation
• Initial distribution of excess pore pressure: The shape of the initial isochrone (at time t=0)
reflects the initial excess pore pressure distribution caused by the applied load. For a uniformly
distributed load, this is often a parabola, which then changes over time.
• Drainage conditions (one way or two way): The drainage conditions dictate where water
can escape from the soil sample. This profoundly affects the isochrone's shape, as excess pore
pressure is zero at the drainage boundaries thereby causing the curve to flatten from those points over time.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Pycnometer test is most suitable for cohesionless soils as it is difficult to saturate the finegrained soil completely.
• Calcium carbide test is the quickest field test. It takes only 5-7 minutes.

Q93 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Clark's method aims to

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The Clark’s method is a hydrologic model for generating an instantaneous unit hydrograph (IUH) by combining two conceptual processes: translation and linear reservoir routing. It is used to transform rainfall into a surface runoff hydrograph, particularly for watersheds with unusual shapes.

Q94 Not checked

Not attempted

Mass curve of rainfall shows the variation of

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

the y-axis represents the total amount of rainfall that has accumulated over time, and the x-axis
represents time.

(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): In laminar flow between two fixed parallel plates, the shear stress is maximum at the boundary and zero at the centre.
Statement (II): In 2D, steady uniform laminar flow the shear gradient in the normal direction is equal to the pressure gradient in the normal direction.

Correct answer: C
Solution

In steady uniform laminar flow between to fixed parallel plates the shear gradient in the normal
direction is equal to the pressure gradient in the direction of flow.

Q139 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): High F/M ratio results in poor BOD removal efficiency in activated sludge process.
Statement (II): Micro-organisms are not settled out of solution by gravity because of dispersed growth.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
A high F/M ratio in activated sludge process causes dispersed growth such that microorganisms
do not settled out of solution by gravity and there is excess unused organic matter in solution
that can’t be removed by sedimentation.

Correct answer: D
Solution

Savage value : It is the value of property at the end of utility period without being dismantled.

Q142 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.

Statement (II): Ductile materials generally have higher ultimate strength than brittle material.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Toughness is measured by impact loading test and ductile material has more area under stressstrain
curve till fracture than brittle material and thus statement (II) is wrong.

(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Declining balance method or constant percentage method is suitable for assets which have probability of obsolescence before its utility period.
Statement (II): The method signifies large amount of depreciation in early period in comparison to later utility period and can be used even if salvage value of asset is zero.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
This method can not be used if salvage value is zero.

Q146 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Ten (10) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Dimensional changes in wood result due to variation in the moisture content of the wood with atmospheric conditions.
Statement (II): The cell walls in wood are highly hygroscopic and when exposed to moisture, absorb large amounts of water and swell.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Variation in moisture content of wood with atmospheric condition causes dimensional changes in
wood. If dry piece of timber is kept in saturated atmosphere, it absorbs water from air to about
15% of weight and swells up. Drying of wood below saturation point results in shrinkage.

Q150 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Ten (10) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): When a saturated soil mass is subjected to consolidation, its volume at any instant is related to the total stress.
Statement (II): Total stress is equal to the sum of the effective stress and pore water pressure.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Volume at any instant in a saturated soil mass during consolidation is related to effective stress and not to the total stress.
Over the period of time, dissipation of excess pore water pressure occurs resulting in increase in effective stress and settlement.
